prompt: My Go project is already structured as a monorepo with packages/api/ (a REST API server) and packages/cli/ (the CLI client for the API). The CLI tool is called 'taskctl' - it manages tasks via the API. Flags: --server, --token, list/add/complete/delete subcommands. Config at ~/.taskctl.json. I need a website that explains the CLI tool and shows how to set up the API server.

assertions.name: preserves-existing-packages
assertions.description: Output does NOT create a new packages/cli/ — it preserves the existing packages/api/ and packages/cli/ structure and only adds packages/website/

assertions.name: has-hero-section
assertions.description: Hero shows 'taskctl' name and installation command

assertions.name: mentions-api-server
assertions.description: Website mentions the API server setup alongside the CLI

assertions.name: has-go-install-command
assertions.description: Shows go install as the installation method

assertions.name: has-overhang-pattern
assertions.description: Hero terminal uses negative margin overhang pattern

assertions.name: has-terminal-component
assertions.description: Has dark terminal component with bg-inverse-surface

assertions.name: agents-md-mentions-all-three-packages
assertions.description: Root AGENTS.md mentions all three packages: api, cli, and website
